output.name: pneumothorax
output.description: An abnormal collection of air or gas in the pleural space between the lung and the chest wall that causes partial or complete lung collapse. It may occur spontaneously, secondary to underlying lung disease, or following trauma/iatrogenic injury (e.g., 'left-sided pneumothorax' following a procedure).

output.name: hepatic steatosis
output.description: An abnormal accumulation of triglycerides within hepatocytes, representing the hallmark of steatotic liver disease. On imaging, this is characterized by decreased parenchymal attenuation on CT, increased echogenicity on ultrasound, and signal loss on out-of-phase MRI sequences.

output.name: subsegmental pulmonary embolism
output.description: The presence of a blood clot within one or more of the subsegmental branches of the pulmonary arteries. This finding, often identified on CT pulmonary angiography, represents a distal pulmonary embolism and may be isolated or occur in conjunction with more proximal arterial thrombi.

output.name: enostosis
output.description: A focus of mature compact bone within the cancellous bone, representing a common benign skeletal variation. This finding is typically asymptomatic and radiographically appears as a well-defined, radiopaque lesion with "thorny" margins that blend into the surrounding trabeculae, as seen in cases of a bone island.

name='pneumothorax', 3 synonyms, desc=The presence of air or gas within the pleural space, which can lead to partial o...

output.name: pneumothorax
output.description: The presence of air or gas within the pleural space, which can lead to partial or complete collapse of the lung. Common clinical presentations include spontaneous, traumatic, or iatrogenic etiologies.

results.output_summary: name='hepatic steatosis', 7 synonyms, desc=The abnormal accumulation of triglycerides within hepatocytes, manifesting radio...

output.name: hepatic steatosis
output.description: The abnormal accumulation of triglycerides within hepatocytes, manifesting radiographically as reduced parenchymal attenuation on CT or increased echogenicity on ultrasound. This condition can present in diffuse, focal, or geographic patterns and is frequently associated with metabolic syndrome or alcohol consumption.

results.output_summary: name='meniscal tear', 3 synonyms, desc=A disruption of the fibrocartilaginous tissue of the knee meniscus, typically re...

output.name: meniscal tear
output.description: A disruption of the fibrocartilaginous tissue of the knee meniscus, typically resulting from acute trauma or degenerative changes. Common patterns include longitudinal, radial, horizontal, or bucket-handle configurations.

results.output_summary: name='subsegmental pulmonary embolism', 3 synonyms, desc=An acute or chronic occlusion of a subsegmental pulmonary artery branch by a thr...

output.name: subsegmental pulmonary embolism
output.description: An acute or chronic occlusion of a subsegmental pulmonary artery branch by a thrombus, often originating from the deep venous system. This finding, such as a "left lower lobe subsegmental pulmonary embolism," represents the most distal form of pulmonary embolic disease visible on CT angiography.

results.output_summary: name='enostosis', 2 synonyms, desc=A focus of mature cortical bone located within the cancellous bone, representing...

output.name: enostosis
output.description: A focus of mature cortical bone located within the cancellous bone, representing a benign developmental failure of intramembranous ossification. In clinical practice, this finding is frequently referred to as a bone island.
